title: Fix shell=True command injection risk in merge.py
description: src/agent_takt/cli/commands/merge.py calls subprocess.Popen with shell=True using the test_command string from config.common.test_command. This is the classic command injection pattern. Replace with shlex.split(test_command) to pass a safe argument list without shell=True. All other subprocess calls in the codebase already use safe command lists. Verify the fix handles commands with quoted arguments correctly (e.g. 'uv run pytest tests/ -n auto -q' must continue to work). Add a test that verifies the subprocess is invoked without shell=True.
